$comment: Ratifiable scope contract for the D-Target Isolation Rule. Standalone file by design: handoffs/current-scope.json still holds WS-operations-process-workbench (PR #31 held, awaiting separate merge ruling). This contract is promoted to current-scope.json when the human authorizes the Builder pass. Core-only mode: the work targets AgenticTeam itself, so no profile and no targetRepo apply (per profiles/README.md, omitted targetRepo = AgenticTeam is the target).
objective: Ratify the D-Target Isolation Rule into AgenticTeam standing law and scope its mechanical implementation: constitution text, role-system precondition, scope-template fields, an inert example hook, and docs. AgenticTeam is the control plane only — nothing from AgenticTeam is ever merged, copied, or injected into ExpertMachina or any other product repo unless the user explicitly scopes that as a product change. Agents operate on external targets only through a dedicated isolated copy of that same target repo.
humanApproval: RULE RATIFIED by Robert (robertkonecny@gmail.com), 2026-07-09, in session ('RULING: Ratify the D-Target Isolation Rule into a written AgenticTeam scope contract'). BUILDER AUTHORIZATION: PENDING — implementation starts only on a separate explicit go-order.
constitutionalRule: Target isolation. AgenticTeam is a control plane. It never merges its own files, branches, agents, or governance content into any target product repo. Agents operate on an external target repo only through a dedicated isolated copy of that same target repo, named '<target repo name> copy for EM', on a dedicated feature branch cut from a fetched origin/<default>. The user's canonical target checkout and its main are read-only to agents. Product changes reach the target's main only through a PR inside the same target repo (isolated copy's feature branch -> target main), merged after explicit human authorization. If an isolated copy cannot be created or verified, the workstream does not start — agents never fall back to the canonical checkout.

definitions.controlRepo: AgenticTeam — orchestration, governance, handoffs, gate records. Never a source of product code.
definitions.canonicalTargetRepo: The user's original checkout of a product repo (e.g. C:/Users/Robert/ExpertMachina). Read-only to agents in every workstream.
definitions.isolatedTargetCopy: A disposable git worktree or full clone of the SAME target repo, named per the naming convention. The only place Builder/Sweeper/product edits happen.
definitions.productFeatureBranch: A dedicated branch inside the isolated copy, cut from fetched origin/<default>. Never main/master.
definitions.productPR: A pull request WITHIN the target repo: product feature branch -> target main. The only path product changes take to main.
definitions.forbiddenCrossRepoMerge: Any merge, copy, push, or injection of AgenticTeam branches, files, agents, hooks, handoffs, or governance content into a target product repo. Constitutionally forbidden unless the user explicitly scopes it as a product change in a ratified contract.

namingConvention.location: Sibling directory of the canonical checkout (e.g. C:/Users/Robert/ExpertMachina copy for EM).
namingConvention.note: Path contains spaces — every git/gh invocation must quote it.

preWorkVerification: 1. cwd is the isolated target copy, NOT the canonical checkout
2. Directory name matches '<target repo name> copy for EM'
3. git remote of the copy resolves to the same target repo as the canonical checkout
4. Checked-out branch is a dedicated feature branch, not main/master
5. Canonical target checkout is clean and untouched (git status empty)
6. AgenticTeam repo is clean except authorized handoffs/gate records
failureRule: Any verification check fails -> STOP. Isolated copy missing -> create it (worktree preferred, clone fallback) and re-verify. If it cannot be created or verified, the workstream does not start. NEVER fall back to editing the canonical checkout.

preferredMechanism.first: git worktree add "../<name> copy for EM" origin/<default>, then create the feature branch. Cheap, disposable, shares .git, and structurally cannot check out main while the canonical checkout holds it — the mechanical fix for the 2026-07-09 branch-drift incident (gate backlog item 7).
preferredMechanism.fallback: Full clone, when worktrees are unsuitable (submodule-heavy repos, separate filesystem, or user preference for maximum isolation).

closeoutRule: Merge authorization must identify, in full: owner/repo, PR number, source branch, target branch, head SHA, and the cwd repo identity the merge command will run from. Ambiguous language ('merge PR #x') is invalid as an authorization. Merges execute only via gh from a verified cwd inside the target repo (or its isolated copy), never from AgenticTeam context without the full identification printed and explicitly approved.

forbiddenGlobs: <every other product repo — this workstream never touches one>
handoffs/2026-07-09-*.md (existing history — append new records only)
handoffs/current-scope.json (operations contract stays active until PR #31 is ruled on)
expectedFiles: CLAUDE.md: add the Target isolation constitutional rule (~12 lines, tightens containment)
docs/role-system-v0.1.md: Builder §4.2 gains the 6-point pre-work verification; anti-pattern #11 'agent edits the canonical checkout'; version note (v0.1 amendment or v0.2)
.claude/agents/{builder,sweeper,prototyper}.md: isolation precondition aligned to the doc
.claude/hooks/pretooluse-target-isolation.example.js: NEW, inert — denies Edit/Write when resolved path is a canonical checkout or branch is main/master; allows correctly-named isolated copies

profiles/TEMPLATE/scope-template.json + profiles/expertmachina/scope-template.json: add targetCanonical (read-only ref) and targetWorktree (isolated copy path) fields; preconditions gain the verification checklist
profiles/README.md + .claude/hooks/README.md + README.md: document the rule, naming convention, and activation

proofRequirements.command: fixture dry-run piping a synthetic PreToolUse payload (Edit targeting a canonical-checkout path) into the hook
fixture dry-run with branch=main in the isolated copy
fixture dry-run with a correctly-named isolated copy on a feature branch
proofRequirements.mustShow: allow (no deny decision), literal output pasted

proofRequirements.command: node -e JSON.parse on both scope templates
grep for project names in core files changed
proofRequirements.mustShow: no product-repo policy leaked into core (naming-convention examples in docs are allowed as examples)

implementationScope.rollbackPlan: All changes are governance text plus one inert .example hook — nothing activated, nothing in any product repo. Rollback = single git revert of the implementation commit. Zero runtime blast radius.

downstreamPlan.part: constitution + role-system + agent files + templates + docs
downstreamPlan.model: sonnet — mechanical doc edits; opus only if hook logic proves non-trivial

explicitStatements: This rule affects FUTURE AgenticTeam workstreams only.
It does not require merging, modifying, or touching ExpertMachina now — ExpertMachina is not touched by this workstream in any way.
PR #31 (ExpertMachina feat/operations-process-workbench -> ExpertMachina main) remains HELD until separately ruled on. Nothing in this contract changes its status.

The Claude Code client's merge-modal wording and repo footer are outside AgenticTeam's control; the closeoutRule above is the enforceable substitute. UI wording is a separate feature request to Claude Code, not part of this scope.
stopConditions: THIS PASS: Steward stops after this contract and its handoff note are written — no implementation until humanApproval's Builder authorization is granted

Builder (when authorized): stops when all proofs run with literal evidence and the handoff is written; does not activate the hook (ships .example), does not touch settings.json, does not commit without instruction
Any agent stops immediately if work would touch any product repo
nextRecommendedAgent: human ruling first (authorize Builder pass, and/or promote this contract to current-scope.json); then builder or steward / sonnet per downstreamPlan
